genre_refs: Norman Rockwell; Boy Scouts of America
description: A reproduction of a classic American illustration shared without any added on-image design elements or overlaid text (the agency caption and attribution live in the tweet, not on the image). The painting depicts a young Boy Scout in uniform standing in profile on a grassy rise: he wears a khaki scout uniform with a red neckerchief, knee socks and boots, carries a bedroll/pack, holds a staff and his brimmed campaign hat, and gazes upward and to the left. Behind and above him, rendered in luminous blue cloud, looms the translucent ghostly figure of a Revolutionary/Continental-era soldier (a Minuteman/George-Washington-like figure) with arm outstretched, symbolically watching over him; a faint torch or beacon glows at lower left. The style is painterly, romantic, warm-versus-cool lighting, oil-illustration. This is the Norman Rockwell scouting painting titled in the tweet as 'A Scout is Loyal.' The tweet text ('Remember who you are, American. 250 Years of American Greatness.') repurposes the patriotic artwork for the Department of Labor's nationalist messaging tied to the U.S. semiquincentennial. Because the image itself bears no agency graphic treatment, seals, or slogan text, it is classified as a plain fine-art reproduction rather than a designed meme/poster.
